critic_raw: Including missingness indicators in a credit risk model raises a serious concern about target leakage. Missingness in financial applications can encode downstream processing information — for example, whether a manual review was requested, whether a credit officer added a note, or whether documentation was withheld pending a decision. If any missingness indicator captures information that became available only after the loan decision or during the collection process, it would be a post-outcome variable that is not available at application scoring time. A model using such a feature would have inflated training and test performance that does not reflect real-world predictive ability.
defender_raw: The missingness indicators are computed at application submission time, before any underwriting decision or collection outcome. The study's methods section explicitly states this: all missingness indicators reflect whether specific fields were absent or whether the bureau returned a thin-file code at the moment the application packet was submitted. The model does not use manual-review notes, final approval decisions, or post-decision interventions. The feature-generation timestamps confirm submission-time derivation. The future-quarter holdout evaluates the frozen model on applications scored before any outcome is observed. Missingness is a legitimate predictive signal available at decision time — an applicant who submits incomplete income documentation or has a thin credit file has characteristics relevant to near-term default risk.
adjudication_raw: The critic raises a valid concern about missingness-based leakage in general. The defender demonstrates that the specific concern does not apply here: the missingness indicators are generated at submission time and the timestamp audit confirms no post-outcome information is included. Defense wins.
all_issues_raised: missingness indicator leakage concern — resolved by submission-time timestamp audit
manual-review or post-decision information concern — explicitly excluded by methods
